mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economic activity, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 3-month average growth rate of PAYEMS is positive, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a critical level, it signals an overheating economy and increases the likelihood of central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently above 230 leads to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust retail sales (RSAFS) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies strong consumer demand and purchasing power, which collectively boosts corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods characterized by both above-average RSAFS growth and below-average UNRATE are associated with flat or negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment, any further decline or sustained low level of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals persistent economic weakness or deflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate slower growth and lower corporate profits.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.25 consistently leads to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust consumer and business confidence, suggesting future economic growth and increased demand for construction-related goods and services, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST above 1000 is consistently followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating weakening economic fundamentals and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as profit expectations diminish.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by > 1% month-over-month are consistently followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and sustained payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and higher consumer spending, ultimately supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If periods meeting the UNRATE and PAYEMS growth conditions are consistently followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a robust economic rebound, which boosts corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy normalizes.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth > 3% annualized during a recovery period is consistently followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
